  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For “Clean this up,” do you want (A) light copyediting only (grammar, concision, clarity, no reframing or reordering of contributions) or (B) a substantive rewrite of the abstract (possible reordering, reframing of the thesis/contributions, and tighter claim calibration)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrasing ‘Clean this up’ most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and conciseness edits without altering content.",
    "RX.1": "While polishing, any uncertainties about intended meaning should be surfaced rather than silently inferred.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid inadvertently strengthening novelty or generality claims beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The task must stay within the requested scope of cleanup and avoid content or structural changes unless explicitly authorized.",
    "RX.4": "Wording changes should not introduce interpretations that could mislead readers about evaluation practices or system performance.",
    "RX.5": "Original figures, metrics, and attributions must be preserved so the provenance of claims remains traceable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For “Clean this up,” do you want (A) light copyediting only (grammar, concision, clarity, no reframing or reordering of contributions) or (B) a substantive rewrite of the abstract (possible reordering, reframing of the thesis/contributions, and tighter claim calibration)?

rationale.R1.6: The phrasing ‘Clean this up’ most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and conciseness edits without altering content.
rationale.RX.1: While polishing, any uncertainties about intended meaning should be surfaced rather than silently inferred.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid inadvertently strengthening novelty or generality claims beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The task must stay within the requested scope of cleanup and avoid content or structural changes unless explicitly authorized.
rationale.RX.4: Wording changes should not introduce interpretations that could mislead readers about evaluation practices or system performance.
rationale.RX.5: Original figures, metrics, and attributions must be preserved so the provenance of claims remains traceable.
